briefings.welcome: Commander — I am your Executive Officer. Together we will stand up your campaign on the Azeroth battle map. Answer each question; I will handle the rest.
briefings.knowledge_level:
EXPERIENCE LEVEL
────────────────
· Tactician — your Executive Officer guides you paragraph-by-paragraph.
· Casual — simpler flow; you may skip the OPORD or let AI draft one for you.
briefings.opord_mode:
OPERATION ORDER APPROACH
──────────────────────
· Skip OPORD — jump straight into the fight; no orders required now.
· Use AI — your Executive Officer drafts a five-paragraph OPORD here on the portal.
briefings.tutorial:
EXECUTIVE OFFICER BRIEFING
──────────────────────────

1. OPEN THE CAMPAIGN
   Double-click scripts/Open Campaign Editor.command
   Edit in Campaign Live (EDIT HERE) → save in Google Earth Pro.

2. SYNC YOUR MARKERS
   After saving: scripts/Sync Campaign Board.command
   (or: python3 scripts/sync_campaign_live.py --push)

3. EXPORT YOUR TURN
   scripts/Export Turn.command with your cell role
   (red-cell or blue-cell) for blind play.

4. WHITE-CELL / DISCORD
   Screenshot your initial force laydown to white-cell always.
   Message white-cell for adjudication when recon or combat reveals something.

5. TURN PHASES
   Offensive: recon → move → attack (move and attack may be simultaneous)
   Defensive: recon → move → reinforce

6. COMBAT
   Attacker rolls 1dN where N = attacking force count.
   Example: 50 vs 100 → attacker rolls 1d50 damage.
   White-cell may modify rolls.

7. DAMAGE TRACKING
   Record damage in the placemark name via Properties.
   Delete the marker when the unit is destroyed.

Contact white-cell on Discord for further guidance.
briefings.xo_briefing_web:
XO BRIEFING
───────────

1. OPEN THE CAMPAIGN
   Open your player pack doc_player.kml in Google Earth Pro.
   Edit markers in Campaign Live (EDIT HERE), then save in Google Earth Pro.

2. SYNC YOUR BOARD
   Hosted: right-click Campaign Board NetworkLinks → Refresh (~60s).
   Local: run Sync Campaign Board after saving in GEP.

3. SUBMIT YOUR TURN
   When it is your cell's turn, upload your KMZ on your table page on this portal.
   Use the upload token white-cell issued you.

4. WHITE CELL & DISCORD
   Screenshot your initial laydown to white-cell.
   Message white-cell when recon or combat reveals something.

5. TURN PHASES
   Offensive: recon → move → attack (move and attack may be simultaneous)
   Defensive: recon → move → reinforce

6. COMBAT
   Attacker rolls 1dN where N = attacking force count.
   White-cell may modify rolls.

7. DAMAGE TRACKING
   Record damage in the placemark name. Delete the marker when destroyed.

Contact white-cell on Discord for further guidance.
briefings.warn_o_ai:
WARN O FROM WHITE-CELL (optional)
─────────────────────────────────
Paste the Warn O (warning order) from white-cell / higher headquarters.

This will be included at the top of the AI prompt on the next step when you
copy it. Leave empty if you do not have a Warn O yet.
briefings.warn_o_tactician:
WARN O FROM WHITE-CELL (optional)
─────────────────────────────────
Paste the Warn O (warning order) from white-cell / higher headquarters.

Your Executive Officer will guide you through each OPORD paragraph next.
If you paste a Warn O here, it will stay visible as a reference pane while
you draft each section. Leave empty if you do not have a Warn O yet.
briefings.tactician_intro:
EXECUTIVE OFFICER — OPERATION ORDER
──────────────────────────────────
Commander, I will walk you through each paragraph of your operation order.
Use the Warn O from higher (if provided) as reference on the left while
you draft each section on the right.

Work one section at a time. Confirm when each paragraph is ready.

tactician_sections.title: 1 — Orientation
tactician_sections.briefing:
EXECUTIVE OFFICER
─────────────────
Orientation orients the reader to the area and time.

In the most basic sense, state:
  · Where the fight is (area of operations)
  · When it takes place (time zone / date-time group)
  · Who the key friendly and enemy forces are at a glance

Keep it short — a few sentences that frame the battlespace.
tactician_sections.skeleton:
Orientation
───────────
Area of operations:

Time zone / date-time group:

Key friendly forces:

Key enemy forces:

tactician_sections.title: 2 — Situation
tactician_sections.briefing:
EXECUTIVE OFFICER
─────────────────
Situation describes factors that affect your mission — enemy,
friendlies, terrain, and civilians. Use the Warn O reference
on the left if white-cell issued one.

Cover each item below. Delete prompt lines you do not need.
tactician_sections.skeleton:
Situation
─────────
Enemy:
  Size:
  Location:

Adjacent units:

Civil considerations:

Friendly unit HQ location:

All friendly units under command:

tactician_sections.title: 3 — Mission
tactician_sections.briefing:
EXECUTIVE OFFICER
─────────────────
Mission is the task and purpose — who, what, when, where, why.

Write a clear two-sentence mission statement:
  · Sentence 1 — the task (what you will do)
  · Sentence 2 — the purpose (why it matters)
tactician_sections.skeleton:
Mission
───────
Task:

Purpose:

(Combine into one two-sentence mission statement below)

Mission statement:

tactician_sections.title: 4 — Execution
tactician_sections.briefing:
EXECUTIVE OFFICER
─────────────────
Execution is how you will accomplish the mission.

Include:
  · Commander's intent — purpose, method, endstate
  · Tactical tasks for each unit one echelon down
    (platoons if you command a company, squads if platoon, etc.)
tactician_sections.skeleton:
Execution
─────────
Commander's intent:
  Purpose:
  Method:
  Endstate:

Tactical tasks (subordinate units):
  Unit 1:
  Unit 2:
  Unit 3:

tactician_sections.title: 5 — Administration & Logistics
tactician_sections.briefing:
EXECUTIVE OFFICER
─────────────────
Administration and logistics covers sustainment of your force.

Describe in roleplay terms:
  · Equipment brought to the fight
  · Vehicles (if any)
  · Medical aid / healers available

When you confirm, the wizard appends a Gameplay Admin block
with turn rules, Google Earth workflow, and command scripts.
tactician_sections.skeleton:
Administration & Logistics
──────────────────────────
Equipment:

Vehicles:

Medical aid:

tactician_sections.title: 6 — Command & Signal
tactician_sections.briefing:
EXECUTIVE OFFICER
─────────────────
Command and signal identifies who commands whom and how
forces communicate.

In roleplay terms, address:
  · Higher command (who you report to)
  · Whether reinforcements can be requested
  · How forces communicate on the battlefield

When you confirm, the wizard appends a Gameplay Signal block
covering Discord coordination and turn submission.
tactician_sections.skeleton:
Command & Signal
────────────────
Higher command:

Reinforcements:

Communication (roleplay):
